observations.value: Authentication bypass: POST /session with any non-empty identifier and secret is accepted with HTTP 200 and sets session=ok cookie. For example, POST /session with identifier=wrong@example.com&secret=wrongpassword returns 200 and a valid session. Credential validation is not enforced — only empty/missing values produce a 401.
Session cookie is trivially forgeable: Set-Cookie header from POST /session always sets 'session=ok; Path=/', a static, unsigned, non-secret token with no HttpOnly or Secure flags.

detections.observation_text: Authentication bypass: POST /session with any non-empty identifier and secret is accepted with HTTP 200 and sets session=ok cookie. For example, POST /session with identifier=wrong@example.com&secret=wrongpassword returns 200 and a valid session. Credential validation is not enforced — only empty/missing values produce a 401.
Session cookie is trivially forgeable: Set-Cookie header from POST /session always sets 'session=ok; Path=/', a static, unsigned, non-secret token with no HttpOnly or Secure flags.
